# Preface {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| include: false
source("_common.R")
```

This site is my replication, in **R**, of

> Lusk, J. L., & Schroeder, T. C. (2004). *Are Choice Experiments Incentive
> Compatible? A Test with Quality Differentiated Beef Steaks.* American Journal
> of Agricultural Economics, **86**(2), 467–482.

The authors ran their analysis in LIMDEP. I redid it in R for the AGEC 5045
(Applied Discrete Choice Analysis, NTU 114-2) replication project. I wrote it as
a Quarto book so that the code, the output it produces, and my own comments are
all in one place and easy to follow.

## What is reproduced

| Output | Chapter | Method |
|---|---|---|
| Data preparation | [Data](02-data.qmd) | read `harrison.xls`, reshape to choice format |
| Tables 1–2 | [Descriptives](03-descriptives.qmd) | demographics & choice frequencies |
| Table 3 | [MNL](04-mnl.qmd) | multinomial logit + Swait–Louviere scale test |
| IIA test | [IIA](05-iia.qmd) | universal ("mother") logit LR test |
| Table 4 | [Advanced models](06-advanced-models.qmd) | HEV, RPL (MNP discussed) |
| Table 5 & Figure 2 | [WTP](07-wtp-figure.qmd) | willingness to pay + Poe test; HEV market shares |
| Discussion | [Discrepancies](08-discrepancies.qmd) | what went differently, and why |

## How to read it

The [Data](02-data.qmd) chapter runs for real when the book is built: it opens
the raw spreadsheet and builds the estimation dataset step by step.

The model chapters work a little differently. The heavy models (the
custom-likelihood MNL scale test, the HEV quadrature, the simulated-ML RPL, and
the Poe bootstrap) are estimated by the numbered scripts in `R/`, which save
their results to `output/*.rds`. The book shows that estimation code but loads
the saved results rather than re-running them, so it builds in a few seconds.
The lighter summaries and Figure 2 are still computed on the spot.

My own comments are in coloured callout boxes, so they are easy to tell apart
from the paper's claims and from the raw output.

::: {.callout-note}
## Reproducibility
All code and data are in the
[GitHub repository](https://github.com/JunJie-Chang/ADCA-replication). To rebuild
from scratch, run the scripts in `R/` in order (see
[Reproducibility](09-reproducibility.qmd)) and re-render the book.
:::

If there is one result to remember, it is this: hypothetical choices overstate
what people will really pay. Every qualitative finding of the paper comes through
in my replication. The numbers that do not match exactly are listed and
discussed in the final chapter.
